## What is SIRENE ? {.smaller}

System for the Identification of the Register of Enterprises and Establishments

- [**Sirene**]{.orange} is the French national company registry
- When a company registers:
    - sole authority for issuing [**SIREN**]{.blue2} and [**SIRET**]{.blue2} numbers
    - a [**main activity code**]{.orange} is attributed ([**APE** code]{.blue2}) 
- Milestones and challenges faced:
  - Refactoring of the Sirene [**information system**]{.blue2}  [**Sirene 4**]{.blue}
  - NACE revision ➡️ dual coding in NACE rev 2 and 2.1 
- End of March 2024:
  - Officially switch from [**Sirene 3**]{.orange} to [**Sirene 4**]{.blue}
- Processing **5 million registrations** across 2025–2026 :rocket:

- [**Consequences**]{.orange}: Ideal moment to innovate (but under the constraint!)

## A one-stop shop 

Another key actor: *National Industrial Property Institute*

- [**INPI**]{.orange} is the operator of the **one-stop shop** (Single Window)
- This replaces the old paper-based [**CFE**]{.blue} (Business formality centers)
- It is the [**primary data source**]{.orange} for the Sirene registry
- Implementation of the **PACTE Law (2019)** to simplify the French economy

::: {.callout-note icon=false}

## PACTE law
Since January 1, 2023, all business formalities must be filed online via a [single portal]{.orange}.
:::

## The flow" of formalities

![](../img/flow-formalities.png){fig-align="center"}

## The administrative landscape

- Siren number: company directory identification system
- Principal activity code (APE)
- Classification on a daily basis
- Different administrations 
- Different information systems
- Requirements: quick, responsive and flexible to updated instructions
